title: Drop In Centers - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Drop In Centers 25 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Sexual Assault Issues p In Centers Sexual Assault Issues BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in space for social activities. Offers assistance with resume building, help to quit smoking, and access to support groups for substance use issues and parenting. 155 S Empire Boulevard Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Individuals and families with minor children in substance use disorder recovery Hours: Monday/Thursday 8am-8pm, Tuesday-Wednesday/Friday 8am-5pm, Saturday 1pm-4pm, Sunday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 756-3111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHURCH @ THE PARK SAFE PARKING C@P SERVICE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a place for people to take showers, do laundry, access case management services, and be indoors. 2640 Portland Road NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 18-24 Hours: Monday-Friday noon-5pm Email (971) 375-7491 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE CLACKAMAS COUNTY TRANSITION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers access to care and case management services providing assistance with such services as SNAP enrollment, 12 step meetings, mental health and substance use assessments, cognitive therapy, finding employment and/or housing, parenting class referrals, GED courses, and hygiene items. Also offers on-site distribution and training in usage of Naloxone. 2219 Kaen Road Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Adults with involvement in the criminal justice system Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 722-6199 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CORVALLIS DAYTIME DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Drop-in center providing a space to socialize, have coffee and snacks, receive mail, phone charging, employment support, information from local health and social organizations, and counseling. Provides assistance with acquiring clothing and shoes; personal hygiene items; medical and dental fees/services; and can assist with transportation needs. Also offers navigation services such as applying for SNAP; completing, obtaining, or replacing official documents like driver's license and birth certificates; access to health providers; housing assistance wait lists; and obtaining a GED. 530 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Homeless and low-income individuals and families; individuals with disabilities Hours: Basic needs: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Social Services support: Monday-Friday noon-2pm by appointment Email (458) 233-5327 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH DESCHUTES COUNTY MENTAL HEALTH THE DROP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a safe and supportive space for youth and young adults. Services include computers and wifi, charging stations for devices, games, music, movies, laundry, information on social services, coffee and snacks, and a quiet corner with activities. 1340 NW Wall Street Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Ages 14-29 Hours: Monday/Wednesday 2pm-5:30pm, Tuesday 5pm-6:30pm, Friday 1pm-4:30pm (541) 797-3623 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV DAY CENTER HIV SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center serving hot breakfast and lunches, access to phones and computers, and recreational activities such as art therapy, games, and video-based media. Also provides clothing, showers, mail services, and hygiene supplies. Guests have access to laundry services, massages, acupuncture, foot care, and haircuts on a sign-up basis. 2941 NE Ainsworth Street Basement Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Individuals who are HIV-positive and have incomes no greater than 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday 1pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 460-3822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH PORTLAND COMMUNITY MEALS AND PANTRY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides community meals, a food pantry, clothing, and personal hygiene and grooming products. Also offers a drop-in resource room. 909 SW 11th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Meals and Resource Room: Monday/Thursday 2pm-4pm Food Pantry: Monday/Thursday 2pm-3:30pm Email (503) 228-7465 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES YOUTH SERVICE CENTER AND DROP IN OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with street outreach and individual case management. Offers support, resources, referral help, and physical items. Case management and goal setting can be set up to help an individual name and work towards future goals. Family mediation is also available for families needing to work through a specific conflict. Financial help and referrals for youth needing to do laundry, obtain interview clothes, or obtain a birth certificate, Social Security card, ID, CPR/First Aid, or Food Handlers certification is also available. 1025 Pacific Boulevard SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 10-24. Individuals from outside the service area will not be turned away. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday by appointment Email (800) 901-2904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MAYBELLE CENTER COMMUNITY ROOM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a drop in center for recreation and socialization, including games, parties, art classes, and more. Also provides guidance and advocacy for members to help navigate social services. 121 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Must be a member to access the Community Room. Members must meet the following requirements: -Currently housed in or around the Downtown Portland area -Self-identify as low-income -Age 18 and older Hours: Monday/Friday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10:30am-2:30pm Email (503) 241-7374 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL THE GLORIA CENTER MONTHLY DROP IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to showers and laundry facilities, hygiene items, a hot meal, warming and cooling supplies (blankets, tarps, hats, gloves, socks, hand and toe warmers, hydration packets, cooling towels, sunscreen, and lip balm), and connections to community resources. 2505 W 7th Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: January-October Last Friday of the month 9am-3pm, hot meal noon-3pm November-December 3rd Friday of the month 9am-3pm, hot meal noon-3pm Email (541) 298-5131 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY SANTIAM OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in resource center offering one-time financial assistance for electric assistance and rent assistance through the Medicaid HRSN program, mail services, hygiene kits, Veterans services, and resource navigation for individuals who are homeless or at risk of homelessness. Also conducts housing assessments for the ARCHES housing program. 280 NE Santiam Boulevard Mill City, OR 97360 Eligibility: Drop-in Center: Unrestricted Housing services: Must be either homeless or at risk of becoming homeless Rent and electrical assistance: OHP members who meet all of the following -Age 65 or older, children age 0-5, pregnant or gave birth in the past 12 months, OR anyone with a qualifying health condition -Have a household income 30% or less of the average yearly income where the applicant lives -Need support staying in housing -Have a lease or written rental agreement -Lack resources to prevent homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Saturday 10am-5pm Email (971) 332-5676 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HEALTH, OUTREACH, SHELTER, AND TRANSITIONS PROGRAM HOST RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with access to clothing, bus passes, meals, phone and internet access, and specialized information and referral. 1115 Liberty Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Age 18-24 and are at risk or experiencing homelessness Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Email (503) 588-5825 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OASIS HOUSE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a daytime drop-in center. Services include hygiene, meals (to-go options available), pet care (including dog and cat food, flea and tick medicine, and toys), a clothing closest (each person is allowed 25 items and one coat), and bike repair. Services are not guaranteed and depend on available donations. 802 SW 5th Street Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Bike repair: Must be an individual experiencing homelessness and the bike their only means of transportation All other services: Unrestricted Hours: -Hygiene: Monday 9am-4pm -Dinner: Tuesday 4:30pm-6pm -Pet care: Wednesday 9am-4pm -Clothing: Thursday 9am-4pm -Bike repair: Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 709-6792 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC THE HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a recovery-oriented drop-in center where participants can sit, have a snack, and connect with others. Services include case management and information and referral to community resources, job finding assistance and employment support. Offers a variety of support groups that cover topics such as wellness, Medication Assisted Treatment (MAT) Education, and making connections. Assist with low-barrier access to treatment services and provides peer support for substance-related issues. Substance use and mental health assessments can be done by telehealth. Provides referrals for MAT. Distributes naloxone and offers education in partnership with other agencies. 404 SW 5th Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-5pm, Wednesday 9am-12:30pm and 3pm-5pm (541) 916-5723 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS FALLS CITY RESOURCE CENTER RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource drop-in center with comprehensive information and referral. 200 Parry Street Falls City, OR 97344 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (971) 399-3121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS MONMOUTH RESOURCE CENTER RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource drop-in center with the following services: comprehensive information and referral, food boxes, a proof of homelessness document for a free Oregon identification card, prescription expense assistance, hygiene items, diapers, formula, transportation passes, and laundry passes. Assistance is based on available funding and is not guaranteed. 1407 Monmouth Independence Highway Monmouth, OR 97361 Eligibility: Unrestricted Transportation passes: Once per month for medical appointments, job interviews, or other appointments supported by documentation Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (503) 751-1644 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PORTLAND IMMIGRANT RIGHTS COALITION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and services to protect and empower the immigrant community including a hotline to report local Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) sightings, assistance with locating loved ones who have been detained or deported, education on immigrant rights including free Know Your Rights fliers and handouts, drop-in resource center, and information and referral to additional resources such as legal aid or accompaniment to check-ins at the ICE office. Also offers volunteer opportunities. 4122 S Moody Avenue Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 7:45am-noon Hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (888) 622-1510 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RAHAB'S SISTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center with access to community meals, a clothing closet, naloxone and fentanyl test strips, safe sex supplies, and access to other community providers. 232 SE 80th Avenue Portland, OR 97215 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as women, transgender, or non-binary Hours: Wednesday 2pm-4pm, Thursday 1pm-4pm, Friday 5pm-8pm Email (971) 208-3176 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center offering lunch, food boxes, laundry, showers, computers with internet access, assistance with housing applications and cover letters, and temporary mailing addresses. May also provide pet food, hygiene supplies, and grab and go foods as supplies allow. 501 3rd Street La Grande, OR 97850 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Wednesday 9am-3pm, Friday 10am-2pm (541) 962-7994 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIVER CENTER LEBANON DROP-IN CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community drop-in center and breakfast. Volunteer and food donations are accepted. 3000 S Santiam Highway Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 9am-10:30am Email (541) 451-1271 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROSE HAVEN ROSE HAVEN DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that assists with food, showers, laundry facilities, clothing, hygiene items, diapers, pet food, and temporary mailing addresses. Advocates are available to help connect individuals to resources and provide emotional support. Outdoor gear provided when available. 1740 NW Glisan Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Women, transgender, non-binary, and gender-expansive adults and their minor children Hours: -Monday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-noon for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am -Tuesday/Thursday 8am-3:30pm for shelter, services from 8:30am-11:30am and 1pm-3:30pm -Wednesday 1pm-3:30pm for guests with minor children only Email (503) 248-6364 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support for survivors of past and present sexual assault, abuse, and harassment and for friends and family of survivors. Services include a crisis line, medical response and legal advocacy, support groups, peer support, community education and outreach for prevention, and information and referral. All services are confidential. 1902 Jefferson Street Suite 1 Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault, abuse, and harassment, and their friends and family members Hours: -Crisis line, advocacy, legal, and medical response: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -All other services: Hours vary (844) 404-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center, domestic and sexual violence advocacy, culturally specific LGBTQ2+ and Latinx services, relocation assistance, transportation to a safe location, accompaniment to medical or forensic exams, assistance applying for Crime Victims Compensation, and information and referral to community agencies. Provides educational programs. These programs focus on raising awareness about the dynamics of domestic and sexual violence, understanding trauma, and promoting prevention strategies within the community. Currently accepting volunteers. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Advocacy Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm, Wednesday 9am-noon, closed 1st Wednesday of every month. Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE ROSEWOOD INITIATIVE WELLNESS WEDNESDAYS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about internal programs, one-on-one resource navigation, workforce navigation (resumes, job search, and more), digital navigation (assistance with using computers or iPads), TriMet Low Income Fare signups, and a food pantry. 14127 SE Stark Street Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Wednesday 10am-3pm Email (503) 208-2562 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TIDES OF CHANGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual assault helpline and resource drop-in center. Services include counseling, support groups, advocacy, transportation, health advocacy, rental assistance, accompaniment services, protection order assistance, 24-hour crisis intervention, limited financial assistance, and a hotline. 1902 2nd Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Individuals who are the victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, trafficking, or dating violence Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 10am-4pm, Wednesday noon-4pm Email (503) 842-9486 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAY AREA FIRST STEP INC RESOURCE DEPARTMENT CHURCH @ THE PARK C@P SERVICE CENTERS CLACKAMAS COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE CLACKAMAS COUNTY TRANSITION CENTER CORVALLIS DAYTIME DROP-IN CENTER CORVALLIS DAYTIME DROP-IN CENTER DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH THE DROP ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV SERVICES FIRST BAPTIST CHURCH PORTLAND COMMUNITY MEALS AND PANTRY JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH MAYBELLE CENTER COMMUNITY ROOM MID-COLUMBIA COMMUNITY ACTION COUNCIL MONTHLY DROP IN CENTER MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY SANTIAM OUTREACH COMMUNITY CENTER NORTHWEST HUMAN SERVICES HOST RESOURCE CENTER OASIS HOUSE OASIS HOUSE OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC THE HUB POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS RESOURCE CENTER POLK COUNTY RESOURCE CENTERS RESOURCE CENTER PORTLAND IMMIGRANT RIGHTS COALITION PORTLAND IMMIGRANT RIGHTS COALITION RAHAB'S SISTERS RAHAB'S SISTERS RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER RIGHT TRACK RESOURCE CENTER RIVER CENTER LEBANON DROP-IN CENTER ROSE HAVEN ROSE HAVEN DAY CENTER SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES OF LANE COUNTY SEXUAL ASSAULT SUPPORT SERVICES THE HARBOR THE HARBOR THE ROSEWOOD INITIATIVE WELLNESS WEDNESDAYS TIDES OF CHANGE TIDES OF CHANGE
